title: Bridge Essay: Framing as a mechanism to overcome the temptation of bad habits.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** The mechanical mind — neuroscience of automaticity revealing how we become trapped in behavioral loops.

**Framing as amechanism to overcome the temptation of bad habits.**. Frontiers in behavioral neuroscience.. PMID: 41234533. DOI: 10.3389/fnbeh.2025.1683756. Behavioral neuroscience generally conceives of habits as under stimulus-response control, and distinguishes habits from goal-directed behavior based on their insensitivity to outcome value (features of automaticity). However, the everyday meaning of "bad habits" is applied primarily to behaviors that are compelling, in part, because of their anticipated outcome value.
